# Signal — Org Namespace Spec

**Namespace**: `org`
**Purpose**: Walk repos, generate org structures, instantiate roles, run governance reviews

---

## org.yaml — The Org Configuration Contract

The central artifact connecting all org skills. `org-scan` produces a draft.
User edits and confirms. All other org skills read from it.

```yaml
# org.yaml — org configuration contract
# Generated by org-scan, confirmed by team, used by org-build / org-rob / org-pr

version: "1"
workspace: my-product               # repo/product name

# ── Exec office ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
exec_office:
  name: BRIDGE                      # team chooses: SPIRE, APEX, BRIDGE, COMMAND...
  roles:
    - cto                           # team names their own exec roles
    - vp-product
    - chief-of-staff
    - s1-reliability                # S-offices or equivalent (optional)
    - s2-velocity
  committees:
    - name: Management Committee
      cadence: biweekly
      chair: cto
      members: [all-group-leads]
    - name: Architecture Board
      cadence: monthly
      chair: cto
      members: [all-area-architects]

# ── Org shape ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
shape: divisional                   # flat | divisional | matrix | pod | tribe

# ── Pivot mode ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
pivot: directory                    # directory | concept | service | domain
pivot_root: src/                    # where to start scanning (for directory pivot)

# ── Standard roles ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Every team gets these automatically — no need to list per team
standard_roles:
  - pm
  - tpm
  - testing
  - security

# ── Level vocabulary ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
levels:
  vp: "Vice President"
  dir: "Director"
  mgr: "Sr Manager"
  ptr: "Partner"
  pnl: "Principal"
  sr: "Senior"

# ── Groups (divisions / tribes / pillars / clusters) ──────────────────────────
# Groups are first-class — they can be nested arbitrarily deep
# Each group can have its own shared roles and sub-groups
groups:
  - name: Platform
    type: division                  # division | tribe | pillar | program | cluster | pod
    lead_role: director             # role that leads this group
    shared_roles:                   # roles shared across all teams in this group
      - architect                   # group-level architect
      - perf-stress                 # cross-team perf role
    teams:
      - area: compiler
        dir: src/compiler/          # for directory pivot
        concept: "IR lowering and code generation"  # for concept pivot
        specialized: [type-system, codegen, optimizer, dragon]
        lead_level: pnl             # team lead level
      - area: runtime
        dir: src/runtime/
        specialized: [agents, memory, pipelines, providers]
      - area: pacts
        dir: src/pacts/
        specialized: [arbiter, formats]

  - name: Product
    type: division
    lead_role: director
    shared_roles:
      - architect
      - ux
    teams:
      - area: suite
        dir: src/suite/
        specialized: [cli, tui, sdk, governance, maker, developer]
      - area: craft
        dir: src/craft/
        specialized: [smith, dev, deploy, optimizer]

  - name: Services
    type: division
    lead_role: manager
    teams:
      - area: flows
        dir: src/flows/
        specialized: [emitter, connectors, triggers, expressions]
      - area: admin
        dir: src/admin/
        specialized: [solutions, versioning, operations, integrity]
      - area: flash
        dir: src/flash/
        specialized: [conduit, schema, transport, import]

# ── Sub-groups (nested) ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# A team can have sub-groups if it's large enough
# Product.suite might have UI sub-group and Backend sub-group
# Use sparingly -- prefer flat teams

# ── Committees ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Committees are auto-generated from groups + standard roles
# or defined explicitly here
committees:
  - name: Product Review (PR)
    cadence: weekly
    scope: product              # which group this committee covers
    chair: vp-product
    participants: [all-pms, all-architects]
    artifact: pr-{date}.md

  - name: Service Review (MSR)
    cadence: monthly
    scope: services
    chair: director-services
    participants: [all-tpms, sre, security]
    artifact: msr-{date}.md

  - name: Shiproom
    cadence: on-demand
    scope: all
    chair: cto
    participants: [all-leads, tpm, security, compliance]
    artifact: ship-decision-{topic}-{date}.md

# ── ROB stages ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
rob_stages:
  - stage: leadership
    doc: leadership-briefing.md
    roles: [exec_office]
  - stage: teams
    doc: team-review-{area}.md
    roles: [per_team]
  - stage: pm
    doc: pm-cross-alignment.md
    roles: [all-pms]
  - stage: tpm
    doc: tpm-risk-register.md
    roles: [all-tpms]
  - stage: arch-board
    doc: architecture-board.md
    roles: [Architecture Board committee]
  - stage: exec-office
    doc: exec-review.md
    roles: [exec_office]
```

---

## Skills

### org-scan
Walk a repo and produce a draft `org.yaml`.

**Pivot modes:**
- `directory`: each top-level directory under `pivot_root` → candidate team area
- `concept`: analyze what code DOES (state management, rendering, data sync) → conceptual teams
- `service`: each deployed service / package → team
- `domain`: group by product domain (Data / API / UI / Infra)

**Output**: draft `org.yaml` with:
- Detected team areas (with `?` markers for uncertain ones)
- Suggested grouping structure
- Suggested standard roles
- Exec office placeholder (name = `?`, roles = `?`)
- Estimated headcount by area (based on code surface area)

**Interactive confirmation**: org-scan does NOT write `.craft/roles/`. It produces the draft `org.yaml` for human review.

---

### org-build
Read `org.yaml` and generate the full org.

**Outputs:**
1. `org-chart.md` — ASCII box diagram in craftworks format, headcount by group/area, level distribution
2. `.craft/roles/{area}/{role}.md` — typed role file per role (standard + specialized per team, shared per group, exec office)

**Role generation:**
- Standard roles: same template for every team (orientation/lens/expertise vary by area context)
- Specialized roles: fully generated from the team's `specialized:` list
- Shared group roles: generated once, scoped to the group
- Exec office: generated from `exec_office.roles`

**Inertia advocate**: always generated as a role in every team. Its job is to challenge every proposal with why doing nothing is sufficient.

---

### org-rob
Run one or all ROB stages.

**Usage:**
```
/org-rob leadership           → stage 1: exec briefing
/org-rob teams                → stage 2-3: all team self-reviews
/org-rob pm                   → stage 4: PM cross-alignment
/org-rob tpm                  → stage 5: risk register + go/no-go
/org-rob arch-board           → stage 6: architecture board
/org-rob exec-office          → stage 7: exec review + S-office missions
/org-rob all                  → all stages in sequence
```

Reads `org.yaml` for role selection. Loads `.craft/roles/` for role personas. Outputs stage documents in ROB format.

---

### org-pr
Run a PR through the org.

Reads `.craft/roles/` → selects reviewers based on files changed:
- Changed `src/compiler/` → compiler roles review
- Changed `src/security/` → security + compliance roles review
- Changed `src/suite/cli/` → cli + ux roles review

Output: structured review with per-role findings, P1/P2/P3 severity, consensus analysis, go/no-go.

---

### org-scan
(see above)

---

### org-committee
Simulate a named committee from `org.yaml` committees.

```
/org-committee "Product Review"    → run PR committee
/org-committee "Shiproom"          → run ship decision
/org-committee "Architecture Board" → run arch review
```

---

### org-roles
Generate typed role files for a specific area (not the full org).

---

### org-chart
Generate or update the org chart diagram from `org.yaml`.

---

## Grouping hierarchy

```
Exec Office (BRIDGE / SPIRE / APEX)
└── Division / Tribe / Pillar (groups of teams)
    └── Team (area)
        └── Sub-group (optional, for large teams)
            └── Role
```

Depth is unbounded — `org.yaml` supports arbitrary nesting via `sub_groups:` inside any group.

Each level can have:
- Its own shared roles
- Its own committee
- Its own ROB scope (`/org-rob pm --scope product` runs only the Product division)
